concerns.text: The plan explicitly states the study is 'observational' and findings will be framed as 'associational' (research.md, 'Causal Inference' section). However, the project title and User Story 1 claim to 'Quantify the Impact' of GB character on diffusivity. In materials science, 'impact' implies a causal mechanism (GB structure drives diffusion rates). An observational design with confounding variables (e.g., local stress, impurity segregation, temperature history) cannot isolate the causal effect of GB character alone. The methodology lacks a causal identification strategy (e.g., instrumental variables, propensity score matching, or controlled simulation design) to support the 'impact' claim, risking a correlational design used to imply causation.

The plan sets a hard minimum n=500 (FR-006) but admits 'No power calculation is performed a priori' (research.md, 'Sample Size & Power'). For a regression model with multiple predictors (misorientation, plane normal, Sigma, temp, composition, geometric descriptors), n=500 may be insufficient to detect the specific effect sizes expected in GB diffusion (often subtle) with adequate power (0.80). Without a power analysis based on expected effect sizes and variance, the 'n=500' threshold is arbitrary and may lead to Type II errors (false negatives) where real effects are missed, or overfitting if the effective degrees of freedom are high.

The plan acknowledges collinearity between misorientation angle and Sigma value (research.md, 'Predictor Collinearity') and proposes reporting Mutual Information (MI) and framing the relationship as 'descriptive'. However, it does not address how this collinearity will be handled in the XGBoost model training or interpretation. High collinearity can destabilize feature importance estimates (SHAP values) and make it impossible to attribute predictive power to specific descriptors. The methodology needs a concrete strategy (e.g., regularization, feature selection, or dimensionality reduction) to ensure the 'quantification' of individual descriptor impacts is valid.
